text.title: Rails 7+ Backend Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the production-grade Rails 7+ methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Exactly one `ServiceResult` class MUST exist at `app/services/service_result.rb`. Public API: `success?`, `failure?`, `value`, `errors`. CI MUST fail if a second `class ServiceResult` definition is detected.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent slightly different `ServiceResult` shapes per file; divergent shapes force controllers to handle multiple cases.

rule.statement: Every Service Object MUST expose a `call` instance method (or `.call` class method) returning the shared `ServiceResult`. Constructor accepts `params:` and `current_user:` keyword args.
rule.rationale: Consistent interface makes every service discoverable + testable the same way; namespaced services (`Users::CreateService`) keep the directory navigable.

rule.statement: Mail, webhooks, search-index updates MUST fire from `after_commit` callbacks, an outbox, or chained Sidekiq jobs. `deliver_later` / external HTTP inside `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `deliver_later` inside a transaction enqueues before commit; rollback leaves the worker running against a non-existent record.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST call `params.require(...).permit(...)` and pass the result to the service. Services MUST NOT touch `params` or HTTP globals.
rule.rationale: Strong Parameters enforce an allowlist at the HTTP boundary; services receiving raw `params` reintroduce mass-assignment risk.

rule.statement: Sidekiq jobs MUST accept primitives (IDs, strings, hashes of primitives). ActiveRecord objects MUST NOT be passed as arguments. Jobs declare `sidekiq_options retry: N, dead: true` + a backoff strategy.
rule.rationale: AR objects serialise as YAML and load a stale row on dequeue; passing IDs ensures the worker fetches latest state.

rule.statement: Migrations containing `remove_column`, `rename_column`, `change_column_default(...)` MUST require explicit human approval before `db:migrate` in any shared environment. CI MUST flag destructive migrations in the PR description.
rule.rationale: Irreversible migrations cannot be rolled back; agents generate destructive migrations without verifying column data.
